tools.name: apivideo_list_videos
tools.description: List videos in the account, with optional filters (title, tags, metadata, description, liveStreamId) and sorting. Read-only. GET /videos.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video
tools.description: Get a single video object by id (title, assets, player URL, status flags). Read-only. GET /videos/{videoId}.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video_status
tools.description: Get encoding and ingest status for a video (upload progress, encoding qualities, playability). Read-only. GET /videos/{videoId}/status.

tools.name: apivideo_list_live_streams
tools.description: List live streams in the account, with optional filters (streamKey, name) and sorting. Read-only. GET /live-streams.

tools.name: apivideo_get_live_stream
tools.description: Get a single live stream object by id (stream key, RTMP ingest, player URL). Read-only. GET /live-streams/{liveStreamId}.

tools.name: apivideo_list_captions
tools.description: List caption tracks for a video (language, source URL, default flag). Read-only. GET /videos/{videoId}/captions.

tools.name: apivideo_list_chapters
tools.description: List chapter tracks for a video. Read-only. GET /videos/{videoId}/chapters.

tools.name: apivideo_list_players
tools.description: List customized player themes in the account. Read-only. GET /players.

tools.name: apivideo_list_webhooks
tools.description: List webhooks configured in the account, optionally filtered by event name. Read-only. GET /webhooks.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video_analytics
tools.description: Aggregated play analytics for videos over a date range, grouped by a dimension. Read-only. GET /analytics/videos/plays.

tools.name: apivideo_get_live_stream_analytics
tools.description: Aggregated play analytics for live streams over a date range, grouped by a dimension. Read-only. GET /analytics/live-streams/plays.

tools.name: apivideo_create_video
tools.description: WRITE. Create a new video object. If `source` (a public video URL) is provided, api.video ingests the video from that URL — recommended for hosted use, since raw file bytes cannot stream through MCP. POST /videos.

tools.name: apivideo_update_video
tools.description: WRITE. Update fields on an existing video. Only the provided fields are changed. PATCH /videos/{videoId}.

tools.name: apivideo_delete_video
tools.description: WRITE. Permanently DELETE a video and all its assets. This cannot be undone. DELETE /videos/{videoId}.

tools.name: apivideo_create_live_stream
tools.description: WRITE. Create a new live stream, returning a stream key and RTMP ingest URL to broadcast to. POST /live-streams.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: apivideo_get_video, apivideo_get_live_stream, apivideo_list_captions · Private data: apivideo_create_video · Public sink: apivideo_get_video_status, apivideo_create_video, apivideo_create_live_stream · Destructive: apivideo_delete_video.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: apivideo_get_video, apivideo_get_live_stream, apivideo_list_captions · Private data: apivideo_create_video · Public sink: apivideo_get_video_status, apivideo_create_video, apivideo_create_live_stream · Destructive: apivideo_delete_video

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
